symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent abdominal pain over ~3 years, related to defecation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain related to defecation over a chronic time course is a core Rome IV feature of IBS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Altered bowel habit — alternating constipation and diarrhoea (mixed subtype)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Change in stool frequency and form defines the mixed subtype of IBS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal bloating with visible distension, worse with stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gaseous bloating and distension are characteristic of functional bowel disorders and stress-triggered colonic spasm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear mucus per rectum with sensation of incomplete evacuation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucus per rectum and tenesmus-like incomplete evacuation are recognised IBS features (mucus without blood).

symptoms.symptom_name: Still passing stool and flatus; no vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued passage of stool and flatus and absence of vomiting argue strongly against a complete mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rectal bleeding and no melaena

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding lowers the probability of IBD and GI malignancy but does not exclude them.
Absence of blood is consistent with a functional disorder; blood would exclude uncomplicated IBS.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unintentional weight loss, no nocturnal symptoms, no fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clean constitutional/alarm screen makes organic disease such as IBD or malignancy substantially less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptom-stress correlation with severe health anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stress reactivity of symptoms is characteristic of disorders of gut-brain interaction; comorbid anxiety is common in IBS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-rigid abdomen; tympanic to percussion; no guarding, rebound or peritonism; active normal-pitched bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft non-rigid abdomen with active normal-pitched bowel sounds and no peritonism argues strongly against obstruction or perforation — but this is a sealed clinician examination the telehealth AI cannot obtain and must not assume.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal baseline bloods and negative coeliac serology

investigation_details.investigation_name: FBC, CRP and coeliac serology (with faecal calprotectin)
investigation_details.expected_result: Normal inflammatory markers and negative coeliac serology (presumed given the 3-year history; not formally documented)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Organic disease (IBD, coeliac) not excluded; risk of premature functional closure.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coeliac serology is part of the standard organic exclusion before a confident IBS diagnosis.
Normal inflammatory markers/faecal calprotectin help exclude IBD; abnormal values would redirect the work-up.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: IBS functional tetrad with clean alarm screen
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recurrent pain related to defecation + altered bowel habit + bloating + an explicit absence of alarm features is the classic positive-diagnosis pattern for IBS and the basis for safe de-escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard baseline organic exclusion for a new/uncharacterised IBS presentation; identifies anaemia, inflammation or coeliac disease.
Helps discriminate IBS from inflammatory bowel disease when alarm features are absent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent abdominal imaging (X-ray/CT) to exclude obstruction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated remotely in a patient still passing stool and flatus with no vomiting and a clean alarm screen; reserved for the escalation pathway if obstruction features develop. Ordering it routinely here would be over-investigation.
Not first-line for uncomplicated IBS with a clean alarm screen; reserved for alarm features or age-based screening thresholds.
